title: Hold UI state in a ViewModel
description: Keep UI state in a ViewModel scoped to outlive Activity/Fragment recreation, not in the lifecycle component itself.
body: Use when a screen has state that must persist across rotation or process-survivable recreation. Do not store screen state in Activity/Fragment fields, and do not reload everything from scratch on every recreation. Actions: put UI state and the logic that produces it in a ViewModel (which survives configuration changes), expose it as observable (StateFlow/LiveData), and let the UI re-bind on recreation; use SavedStateHandle for state that must survive process death. Validation signals include rotating the device and seeing state preserved with no reload. Risk boundary: holding state in the Activity loses it on every rotation and leaks the Activity if a long task outlives it — the ViewModel exists precisely to decouple state lifetime from the UI component's lifetime.
